def: s.m. & adv. Ringing sound, tinkle, jingle, clink, chink (of money); — tinkle-tinkle, clink-clink, chink-chink
s.f. Sounding, ringing, resounding; tinkling, tinkle, &c. ( = ćhanćhan); — tingling, smarting, smart.

ety: S. छनच्छन्न onomat.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To sound, ring, tinkle, clink, chink (as money); to simmer; to sing (as a kettle); — to tingle, smart, pain (cf. jhanjhanānā).
s.f. Sickness, vomiting.
s.m. Deceit, fraud, dishonesty, trick, craft:-ćhand-band, s.m. Trickery, cheating, &c.
adj. Concealed, hidden, clandestine, secret, private, solitary.

adj. & s.m. Praising, flattering; — appearance, look, shape; — wish, desire, pleasure, predilection; will; opinion; meaning, intention, purport, object; self-help; power, subjection; a composition in verse
subs: s.m. An ascetic in appearance only, a pretended or false ascetic; a pretender, a hypocrite.

def: s.m. A sacred hymn; the sacred text of the Vedic hymns; metrical science, prosody; poetical metre; a measure (in music)
subs: s.m. One who steals (another's) verses, a plagiarist
s.f. The measure or feet of verse
ćhando-ga, ćhandog, s.m. A reciter or singer in metre; a chanter of the Sāma Veda

def: s.f. Fastening, tether; trammel; net
s.m. Share, part (among faqīrs).
s.m. = ćhāṅdā, q.v.
adj. Deceitful, dishonest, tricky; — s.m. A deceiver, trickster, &c.
v.t. To fasten, tie, bind, tether, trammel

ety: ćhāṅḍ˚ = Prk. छड्ड(इ) = S. छर्द(ति), rt. छृद्
def: v.n. To vomit, to spew; — to let go, to release, &c. ( = ćhoṛnā, q.v.).
v.n. To be fastened, be tied, be bound, &c.
v.n. To vomit, &c. ( = ćhāṅḍnā).
v.n. To vomit, &c. ( = ćhāṅḍnā).
s.m. See s.v. ćhand, 'metre.'
s.m. The doctrine of the Ćhando-gas or Udgātr̤i priests contained in a Brāhmaṇa of the Sāma-veda; — the Vedas in general.
adj. & s.m. Having six fingers; — one who has six fingers.
s.f. Shade, shadow; a reflected image, reflection
s.f. Shade, shadow; a reflected image, reflection

ety: ćhaṅhā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Shadiness.
adj. Appertaining to a shadow, &c.; shadowy.
s.f. Thatch, roofing.
adj. Momentary, temporary, transient, fleeting, perishable, inconstant, uncertain.
adj. (f. -ī), Full of small stones or pebbles, gritty.
s.f. Causing to strain; concentrating, concentration.
v.t. To cause to strain; to concentrate.

ety: ćhān˚ = Prk. छन्न(इ), or छन्ने(इ), fr. S. स्यन्न (p.p.p. of rt. स्यन्द्)
def: v.t. To sift; to strain, to filter; — to search minutely, to investigate; to canvass; to explore
subs: v.t. To sift, &c. ( = ćhānnā); — to cull, to select
v.t. To sift, to search closely, to rummage, to explore; to canvass.

def: v.n. & s.m. See چهننا ćhannā.
s.m. A strainer, a sieve; a cloth through which anything is sifted (syn. ćhalnā).
s.m. A cup of bell-metal, &c. (that gives out a ringing or tinkling sound).
part. Covered, concealed, hidden.

v.n. To be strained, be percolated; to be sifted, be cleaned; to be shed; to be concentrated (upon, -par); — to be closely searched, be scrutinized; — to make or find a way through (-se) interstices, openings, or chinks (as the sun through the leaves of trees, &c.); — to be fought out (a quarrel)
s.f. = ćhannā, q.v. (syn. ćhalnī).
s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v.).
s.f. = ćhāṅḍ, q.v.
s.m. = ćhārā, q.v.; — and perf. part. of next.
v.t. & v.n. = ćhāṅḍnā, q.v.
s.f. Vomiting, vomit ( = ćhāṅḍ, q.v.)
subs: To vomit, to disgorge ( = ćhāṅṭnā, ćhāṅḍnā).

def: s.f. A cutting, clipping, paring, scrap, slip, chip, a small bundle of cuttings (as of thorns, &c.); (ćhāṅṭ), cut or fashion (of clothes); — picking, sorting, selection, excerpt, extract; separating (into classes, &c.); siftings, refuse, rubbish
subs: s.m. = ćhāṅṭ-ćhūṭ, s.f. Cutting off, cutting down; cuttings, slippings, &c., trimming, pruning; — retrenchment; savings, scrapings
To cut or clip off, to trim, &c.; to retrench; to save, lay by.

def: part. adj. = ćhaṭā, q.v.
s.f. = ćhaṅṭwāʼī, q.v.

s.f. A cutting, clipping, paring, scrap, slip, chip, a small bundle of cuttings (as of thorns, &c.); (ćhāṅṭ), cut or fashion (of clothes); — picking, sorting, selection, excerpt, extract; separating (into classes, &c.); siftings, refuse, rubbish
subs: s.m. = ćhāṅṭ-ćhūṭ, s.f. Cutting off, cutting down; cuttings, slippings, &c., trimming, pruning; — retrenchment; savings, scrapings
To cut or clip off, to trim, &c.; to retrench; to save, lay by.

def: v.t. = ćhaṅtwānā, and ćhāṅṭnā, qq.v.

s.m. A cutting, paring, scantling; retrenchment, reduction, saving; — beating or pounding rice to clear it from the husk, husking; husked rice, &c. siftings, refuse; pus, discharge (from a sore or wound; — secundines or after-birth; a detachment on forage (see ćhaṭāʼo).
v.t. = ćhāṅḍnā or ćhāṅṛnā, q.v.

ety: ćhāṅṭ˚ = Prk. छट्ट(इ)? fr. S. त्वष्ट, rt. त्वक्ष्
def: v.t. To cut, clip, pare, crop, lop, prune, trim, dress; to cut off, cut clean through; to cut up (an army, &c.); to knock off; to cut out (clothes); to cut down, reduce, retrench (allowances, expenses, &c.); to abbreviate, abridge, curtail; — to pick, pick out, cull, select, sort, to separate the husk from grain (by pounding it in a mortar), to husk; to sift; to remove (dirt, straws, &c. by some light and smart process); to wash (clothes) slightly (without boiling them); to clean, clear (of weeds, &c.); — to reject; — to discuss (rumour, news, &c.); to chop (words, politics, &c.)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To cut off; to cut out; to cut up, &c.; also = ćhāṅṭnā.

def: v.n. = ćhaṭnā, q.v.
s.f. Separating, picking, sorting, selecting; sifting, cleaning, &c.; price paid for picking, or sifting, or cleaning, &c.

v.t. To cause to be cut, or clipped, &c.; to have or get pruned, or trimmed, &c.; to cause to be separated, or sorted, or picked, or cleaned, &c.; to have or get (rice, &c.) husked by beating or pounding.
s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v.).
s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be strained, to have or get strained or sifted, &c.
adj. Ninety-six.
s.f. = ćhāṅw, ćhāṅʼoṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v.

ety: Prk. छावडओ or छावलओ; S. शाव+ल or र+कः
def: s.m. Young (of an animal), young one ( = ćhāʼoṛā).
v.t. To thatch, &c. ( = ćhānā, q.v.).
s.f. Thatching, roofing, covering; the art of thatching; — huts or barracks for soldiers, a cantonment
subs: 'To thatch a roof'; to make a home for oneself, to take up (one's) abode
To form or make a cantonment
(-meṅ), To canton, to take up quarters (in).

def: s.f. The sound produced by striking water the splashing sound of water.

s.f. Stamp, print, copy; impression (of a stamp, or type, &c.); a seal, a signet; stamp (on coins), mark (on weights and measures to indicate their accuracy), an official stamp in general; sectarial marks representing a lotus, trident, &c., delineated on the body by the worshippers of Vishnu ( = ćhāpā)
subs: ćhāp lagānā (-meṅ), To put a seal or stamp (on), to stamp, seal, print, make an impression or mark (on).

def: s.m. Stamp, print, seal, impression ( = ćhāp); a large wooden seal with which corn in a granary is stamped (also ćhāp); sectarial marks ( = ćhāp); the stamp for making sectarial marks; printing; print, impression; an edition; a press; — a night-attack
subs: s.m. A tax on stamping cloth
s.m. A printing-office; a press; — ćhāpe-ḵẖāna-wālā, s.m. Proprietor of a press
(-meṅ, or -par), To put a stamp or seal (on), to stamp, &c.
(-par), To surprise (an enemy) in a night-attack
To appear in print, to be published; to be exposed or defamed in print

def: s.m. = ćhāpā, q.v.
s.f. The sound produced by striking water the splashing sound of water.
s.f. Printing; impression, edition; the price or cost of printing.
s.m. A printer, a pressman ( = ćhāpne-wālā).
s.f. Splashing, a splash ( = ćhap, q.v.)
subs: s.f. The sound produced by striking water ( = ćhap-ćhap)
To make a splashing noise; to splash.

ety: ćhap+Prk. अक्कओ or आकओ = S. अ+क+कः
def: s.m. = ćhap, or ćhap-ćhap, q.v.

ety: ćhapak˚ = Prk. छंपक्के(इ), fr. S. क्षम्प्+कृ
def: v.n. Water to be dashed or thrown; to be splashed; to be beaten.

ety: caus. of ćhāpnā; — ćhapā˚ = ćhap+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to be stamped, marked, or printed, to get or have printed ( = ćhapwānā).
s.m. A kind of poem in Hindī verse consisting of six hemistichs.
s.m. One who prints cloth, &c. ( = ćhīpī, q.v.).
s.m. A splash (of water = ćhapak); — a net (for catching pigeons, &c.); a cage with a net attached to it.
v.t. To dash or throw (water); to splash.

ety: ćhap˚ = Prk. छंप(इ) = S. क्षम्प(ति), rt. क्षम्प्
def: v.n. To be stamped, be marked; to be printed, be published.
v.t. To stamp, to seal; to print, bring out an impression, to publish
adj. Fifty-six.
s.m. incorr. for ćhappar, q.v.
s.m. A sloping thatch, a thatched roof; a shed, a hut; — tester of a bed); — (fig.) a heavy burden
subs: s.f. Thatching; price of thatching; cultivating, with permanent residence (in a village)
v.t. 'To place on the thatch,' to throw aside as worthless, to lay aside, to care nothing for
(-ko), 'To cleave the roof and give'; to surprise (one) with an unexpected gift; to give in a miraculous manner
'Roof to fall in'; a sudden calamity to befal (one)
ćhappar ḍālnā, To thatch; to make a roof or home (for oneself), to take up (one's) abode, to abide, to tarry
ćhappar-khaṭ, ćhapar-khaṭ, s.m. A bedstead with a tester and curtains.

ety: ćhap, q.v.+rī, Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A puddle, a pool.

ety: ćhāp(nā)+tī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आपि (caus. augment)+ति+इका (i.e. आप्ति का)
def: s.f. A seal or mark of cow-dung placed on a heap of grain on the threshing-floor to prevent its removal.
s.f. Printing; cost of printing ( = ćhapāʼī).
v.t. To get or have printed, &c. ( = ćhapānā, q.v.).

ety: Prk. छारो and छारू; S. क्षारः and क्षारं
def: adj. & s.f. Caustic, corrosive; pungent; acid; saline; — alkali; ashes; dust, earth
subs: s.m. 'A pool of acid or saline mud,' name of a hell.

def: s.f. A mass, a lump; a clod (of earth).
s.f. = ćhāṛ, q.v.
adj. = ćhāl, q.v.
s.m. A shrub, plant, &c. ( = jhāṛ, q.v.)
subs: s.m. A medicinal plant, spikenard; a kind of fragrant moss, &c. (see ćhaṛelā).

def: s.f. Leaving, abandonment ( = tyāg); bank (of a river); land gained from a river, alluvium
subs: s.f. A permit, pass, passport ( = ćhoṛ-ćiṭṭhī).

def: s.f. = ćhaṛ, q.v.
s.f. Spikenard.
s.f. Shaft, pole, pike, staff, flag-staff; a fishing-rod (cf. ćhaṛī); — stem, stalk; stubble.
s.m. An ear-ornament made of pearls.
s.m. Ashes, &c.; — a lump; a large clod.
adj. (f. -ī), Separated, separate, apart; solitary, single, alone ( = ćhaṭwāṅ; ćhaṛīlā)
adj. (f. -ī), Worn, emaciated, thin, lean, spare (man).
adj. = ćharahrā, q.v.
v.n. To be startled (an animal), be scared (syn. bhaṛaknā).
s.f. Jakes, a necessary.
s.f. Sickness, nausea, vomiting, retching
s.f. Sickness, nausea, vomiting, retching
s.f. Sickness, nausea, vomiting, retching

ety: S. छटा; or dim. of ćhār
def: s.f. A lump (of unalloyed silver, or gold, &c.).
s.f. = jhāṛī, q.v.

s.f. A switch, stick, wand, rod, cane, a walking-stick; — a procession of the followers of Shāh Madār; — ćhariyāṅ, pl. A kind of embroidery resembling a branch with twigs and shoots; — trimmings; stripes; — a Hindū marriage ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom playfully beat each other with sticks covered with flowers on the fourth day after marriage (see ćauthī)
subs: s.m. The marshal who walks before people of consequence with the mace or wand of office, a mace-bearer ( = ćob-dār)
s.f. pl. Sticks covered with flowers.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Separate, &c. ( = ćhaṛā, q.v.).
s.m. Spikenard; — a kind of fragrant moss (cf. ćhāṛ-ćhaṛelā).

ety: ćhaṭ(nā)+indā = andā = Prk. अंतओ or अंदओ = S. अन्त+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Separate, alone, single, free, not burdened (a traveller), not loaded (cf. ćhaṭwāṅ; ćhaṛā; ćhaṛīlā).
s.f. pl. of ćhaṛī, q.v.

ety: ćhāṛ˚ = Prk. छड्ड(इ) = S. छर्द (ति), rt. छृद
def: v.t. To let go, release, &c. ( = ćhoṛnā, q.v.); — to vomit ( = ćhāṅṛnā, q.v.).
v.t. To separate (husk from rice, &c.), to beat or pound (rice), to husk; — to beat, chastise.
s.m. Small shot; a charge of shot, a shower of small pebbles, or balls, or nails, &c.
subs: v.n. To speak with animated and overpowering eloquence; — to smoke madak, q.v.
v.t. To load (a gun) with shot; to give (one) a charge of shot, or a shower of pebbles, &c.
v.n. Shot, or small pebbles, &c. to fly about, or be showered; jests to fly about; — to smoke madak.

def: s.f. dim. of ćharrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. छारु; S. क्षार+क; but cf. ćhālā
def: s.m. Blister; ulcer, the thrush (disease).
v.t. To cause husk to be separated (from rice, &c.) by beating or pounding, to have or get (rice, &c.) husked.
adj. Separate, &c. = ćhaṛā (used in comp.)
adj. (dialec.) = ćhiyāsaṭh, q.v.
adj. = ćhiyāsaṭh, q.v.
adj. = ćhiyāsaṭh, q.v.
adj. = ćhiyāsaṭh, q.v.
s.f. Roof ( = ćhat); — adj. Emaciated, thin, feeble.

ety: S. छदि or छदिः rt. छद्
def: s.f. A roof; a ceiling; a ceiling-cloth; — a platform
subs: ćhat pāṭnā, To ceil, to cover the inner roof of a building

def: s.f. = ćhaṭh, q.v.
s.m. A sweetheart, a lover.
s.m. An umbrella; — a broad and prominent chest (like that of an athlete), the breast; — a mushroom, a fungus; — mouldiness.
s.f. A lump, mass; assemblage; number; multitude; a collection of rays of light; light, lustre, splendour, brilliance, brightness, refulgence, a glory; — a straight or continuous line or mark
part. adj. Picked, sorted, selected; — doubly-distilled, out-and-out, consummate (rascal or villain, &c.)
adv. Sixthly, in the sixth place
subs: ćhaṭe-ćha-māhe, adv. 'Once in six months,' now and then.

def: s.f. See s.v. ćhaṭā.
adj. (f. -ī), Snappish, peevish, waspish.
v.t. To cause to be cut or clipped, to get or have pruned or thinned, &c.; to cause to be picked, or sorted, &c.; to have the husk separated from grain; to sift, &c. ( = ćhaṅṭwānā, q.v.).
s.f. The sixteenth part of a ser, two ounces (avoirdupois, nearly).
s.f. A weight of two ounces.
s.m. Picking out; sorting; detaching, separating; clearing grain or rice from the husk ( = ćhaṅṭāʼo, q.v.).

ety: ćhaṭā, p.p. of ćhaṭ-nā+S. क (with w inserted)+लः
def: adj. = ćhaṭwāṅ, q.v.
s.f. The sixth day of the lunar fortnight (cf. ćhaṭhī or ćhaṭī).
adj. (f. -ī), Sixth ( = ćhaṭā, q.v.).
adj. (f. īṅ), Sixth ( = ćhaṭwāṅ, q.v.).

s.f. The sixth day after the birth of a child (on this day the house undergoes a thorough cleaning, the midwife receives her present, the mother and child are bathed, the child is named, and friends are invited to dinner)
subs: s.m. Meats prepared on the sixth day after the birth of a child

def: adj. (f. īṅ), Sixth ( = ćhaṭwāṅ, q.v.).

ety: Prk. छत्ता; S. छत्त्त्रा or छत्त्त्र+इका
def: s.f. (dim. of ćhātā), A small umbrella, a parasol; — the breast, chest, bosom; a breast, dug, bubby; — heart, spirit, courage; fortitude; liberality
subs: 'The heart to be full,' to be affected, be moved or touched
'The breast to heave' (with emotion), to be greatly moved, to weep
'To place a heavy stone on the breast' (to still its heaving), to have patience, endure patiently
(or -pe) sāṅp loṭnā, To burn or be tormented with envy
'To grind pulse on the breast' (of, -kī), to do anything vexing or calculated to pain (one) in his presence
To place the hand on the breast (of, -kī); to press or squeeze the breast (of a woman)
'To lay the hand on the bosom and be quiet,' to grieve or mourn in silence
To hold the breast (in emotion, pain, &c.); also ćhātī-par hāth ḍālnā, q.v.
'The breast to suppurate,' the heart to be sore, to feel very sore or vexed
The chest to be bursting (with pain); the heart to burst (with grief or sorrow); to sympathize
To beat the breast (in sorrow, &c.), to grieve, to lament, to regret, to repent
'To keep under or within the bosom,' to keep very carefully
v.n. To be encouraged, be assured, be satisfied
To still or quiet the breast, to please, to assuage, to comfort
The heart to be quieted; to be pleased, be comforted or solaced
(-kī), To pat on the breast; to encourage, to assure; — to hurt, pain, wound
To set the breast aflame, to cause heart-burn (to), to wound the feelings (of)
To have the heart-burn, to burn with grief, or envy, &c.
or ćaṛh-rahnā, The breasts to be very full or to be distended (with milk)
To press the breast (as a child does in sucking; hence), to suck the breast
adj. Courageous, spirited, brave, valiant; liberal, generous
(-kī), To praise the courage or the fortitude (of)
(-ko), To fold to the breast, to embrace; to fondle
ćhātī-kā pahāṛ, s.m. 'A heavy weight on the chest,' an intolerable burden, an encumbrance, an incubus, a nuisance, a pest
s.m. 'The destroyer of the heart'; an incubus, &c. ( = ćhātī-kā patthar)
To show courage or magnanimity; to show liberality
'The gates of the bosom to burst open,' to speak out loudly or vehemently, to make a great noise; to be consumed with envy, &c.
The breast to swell (in a young woman)
To lament, to grieve ( = ćhātī pīṭnā); — also = ćhātī-se lagānā, q.v.
(-kī), To press or squeeze the breasts (of a woman)
'To walk with the chest thrown forward,' to stalk, to strut.

def: s.f. Hurting, hurt, injury, harm, detriment; destruction, damage.

s.f. The sixth day after the birth of a child (on this day the house undergoes a thorough cleaning, the midwife receives her present, the mother and child are bathed, the child is named, and friends are invited to dinner)
subs: s.m. Meats prepared on the sixth day after the birth of a child

def: s.f. (prov.) = ćhātī, 'breast.'

ety: ćhātī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव = S. आपि (caus. augment)
def: v.t. To raise (a gun) to the shoulder, to point (a gun).

v.n. To be cut, be clipped, be lopped, be pared, be pruned, be trimmed, &c.; to be cut taper, be nibbed (a pen); to be hewn down; to be hacked asunder; to be cut up (as an army, &c.); — to be picked out, be sorted; to be sifted; to be separated, be dispersed; to be cleaned; to separate; to be diminished, be reduced, be retrenched; to diminish; to waste away, become emaciated or thin, to decay (also written ćhaṅṭnā)
s.m. An instrument for separating or sifting, &c., a kind of sieve.

ety: S. छत्त्त्रक+ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: adj. 'Umbrella-shaped' or 'mushroom-shaped'; bunchy, flat.
s.f. Tossing about, fluttering; floundering; agitation, &c. ( = ćhaṭpaṭī).
s.f. Tossing about, floundering, fluttering; flutter, agitation, restlessness; briskness, activity; haste, hurry.
v.n. To toss or tumble about, to flounder, to flutter; to be agitated.
s.f. Tossing about, floundering, fluttering; flutter, agitation, restlessness; briskness, activity; haste, hurry.
s.m. A man of the second or Kshatrī caste ( = ćhatrī)
subs: s.m. A mere Kshatrī, a Kshatrī in name only, a base Kshatrī (a term of abuse).

def: s.m. A scholar, disciple, pupil, student; a tyro, novice.
s.f. A kind of fennel, Anethum sowā; anise; coriander; — a mushroom.
s.m. An umbrella (particularly a large umbrella held over idols and kings); — a cake of cowdung, or a stick, &c. placed on a heap of winnowed corn (in order to avert an evil eye)
subs: s.m. 'Breaking or destruction of the royal parasol,' loss of dominion or empire, deposition; a forlorn condition; widowhood
s.m. 'Lord of the umbrella or parasol,' any king over whom a parasol is carried as a mark of dignity, a Rājā, king, prince
s.m. 'Bearing a parasol or umbrella,' a king, &c. ( = ćhatr-pati)
adj. & s.m. Possessing an umbrella; — one who has an umbrella ( = ćhattrī).

def: s.m. A mushroom.
s.m. The second or military caste of Hindūs; a member of the military or reigning class, a warrior, a soldier.

s.f. A small umbrella, an umbrella ( = ćhātā); covering, canopy, or tester (of a bed); covering or hood (of a carriage, &c.); a small ornamental pavilion generally built over a place of interment, or a cenotaph in honour of a Hindū chief, or a faqīr; the poop of a ship; a bamboo frame (attached to the upper extremity of a long pole, for pigeons to settle upon); — long hairs on the crown of the head
subs: adj. Having a covering, or tester, or hood; covered, canopied, hooded (as a carriage, &c.).

def: adj. & s.m. Bearing or having an umbrella; — one who has an umbrella.
s.f. A woman belonging to the Kshatrī caste; the wife of a Kshatrī.
s.m. A bee-hive, a honey-comb; a group, cluster; — a roofed lane, a covered way or passage.
s.m. A house set apart for the charitable entertainment of strangers, a choltry or resting-place; a shed, an open building.
adj. Thirty-six
subs: s.m. Thirty-six dishes of food ( = ćhattīs vyanjan); a variety of dishes
s.m. An epithet of a Brāhmaṇ considered as possessing all the virtues possible to one of his order
s.m. The thirty-six different kinds of food that are lawful to Brāhmaṇs.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Cunning, artful, crafty; — ćhattīsī, adj. & s.f. Prudish, wanton, lewd; — s.f. A prude, an artful wanton woman, a strumpet (one who knows thirty-six postures in copulation and yet affects modesty and simplicity).
s.m. Artfulness, cunning, craftiness, wiliness.
s.m. A covering placed on a heap of winnowed corn, &c. ( = ćhatr or ćhattar, q.v.).

ety: Prk. छटमओ or छटवँओ; S. षट्+तम+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Sixth ( = ćhaṭhā).
adj. Separated, sorted, picked; select, choice; — rejected, thrown out or aside ( = ćhaṭawwal).
s.m. A house, a dwelling; an arbour, a bower.
s.m. A scrambler.
v.n. To scramble.
s.f. = ćǒhal, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ćǒhalā, ćǒhlī, qq.v.

ety: Prk. छावडओ; S. शाव+ल or र+कः
def: s.m. The young of any animal; — (dialec.) boy, son ( = beṭā).
s.f. = ćhāʼonī, q.v.
s.m. Seasoning, spices with which food is seasoned.
v.t. To throw in seasoning, to season
s.m. A large stack of jawār, or bājrā, &c. collected for fodder; — walking a boundary with a raw cow-skin on the head under a solemn oath to decide correctly.
s.m. Shaving of the head or beard; — the office of barber
s.f. A stack or bundle of fodder.
s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant (from ten to twenty years old) = ćhaunā.
s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅh, q.v.).
s.m. A thatcher.
adj. Sixth ( = ćhaṭhā, q.v.).
s.f. A kind of basket (made of twigs of the jhāʼū or tamarisk tree; syn. ćhabṛā).
s.f. Hide, skin; colour; light, brilliance, lustre, splendour; beauty (see ćhab).
s.m. Young (of an animal), young one, young creature; young tiger (cf. ćhāʼonā).

ety: Prk. छयं or छइअं; S. क्षयः, rt. क्षि 'to destroy'
def: s.f. Loss, waste, diminution, destruction, decay, wasting or wearing away; removal; frailty; mortality; pulmonary consumption, any wasting disease
subs: adj. Causing or promoting decay or destruction, &c.; destructive.

def: s.f. Shade, shadow; a shady place; reflected image, reflection; light, lustre, splendour; colour; — darkness, obscurity; — s.m.(?) An apparition, a spectre; a faint image or resemblance
subs: asylum, protection ( = sāya); — a mode (in music)
s.m. Ascertaining the time by observing the shadow (of an object)
s.m. Ether, the atmosphere or firmament
s.m. A large umbrageous tree (giving abundant shade or shelter)
s.m. Offering up one's shadow (to ward off the influence of evil: this is effected by looking at one's image in a cup of oil, which is then presented to the the ḍakaut, q.v.)
s.m. 'Friend of the shade'; a parasol or umbrella
ćhāyā-vant, adj. Possessing or granting shade, shady, shadowy

def: s.f. = ćhāyā, q.v.

ety: S. क्षय+कः, rt. क्षि 'to dwell,' &c.; cf. also ćhāwā; rt. छद्
def: s.m. (f. ćhaʼī), Race, family, generation; — child, boy (e.g. ab-kī ćhaʼī kī nirālī bāteṅ, 'The language or conversation of the rising generation is extraordinary').

ety: S. क्षययितव्य+कंः, or क्षेय+कः, rt. क्षि 'to destroy'
def: s.m. A victim.
s.f.(m.?) = ćhāyā, ćhāṅ, q.v.
s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.
adj. (f. -ī), Shady, umbrageous, shadowy.
adj. (dialec.) = ćha, q.v.

ety: Prk. छिद्दं; S. छिद्रं, rt. छिद्
def: s.m. A hole, opening, orifice, aperture (cf. next).

s.m. Cutting, cutting off, dividing; (in Math.) divisor, the denominator of a fraction; — a cutting, cut, section, slice, piece; — an incision, cleft, slit; — destruction, dissipation, interruption, vanishing, cessation, deprivation, want, deficiency.
s.m. A small insect of the weevil kind (destructive to corn), Calandra granaria; — the disease in corn occasioned by this insect.
s.m. Anything with which a hole is bored, borer, piercer, splitter.
s.m. Cutting, cutting or lopping off, removing; amputation, mutilation, tearing asunder, splitting; — boring, piercing (e.g. kan-ćhedān, 'ear-piercing'); — a hole, opening, orifice
subs: v.t. To cut, pare, lop off, &c.

ety: ćhed˚ = Prk. छिद्द(इ) or छिद्दे(इ) = S. छिद्रय(ति), fr. छिद्, rt. छिद्
def: v.t. To make a hole in, to bore, pierce, perforate; to cut, pare, cut or lop off
subs: v.t. (intens.) To pierce through and through, &c.

def: adv. (Old H.), In (or at) the end, finally, at length.
v.n. See ćhenā or ćheʼonā.
s.m. = ćhed, q.v.
adj. Tame, domesticated; shrewd, sharp, smart
subs: (ćheka+uktī), s.f. Indirect speech, insinuation, double-entendre, hint, &c.

def: s.f. = ćheṅk, q.v.
s.m. A hole, &c. ( = ćhek, ćhed q.v.).
v.t. = ćheṅknā, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. ćhek.
s.m. (dialec.) A goat ( = ćherā, q.v.).
s.f. A she-goat ( = ćherī, q.v.t.
s.m. The medicinal plant Vernonia (or Conyza) anthelmintica.
s.f. Welfare, happiness (see kshem)
s.m. A fatherless child, an orphan.
s.f. The Brāhmaṇī kite or Coromandel eagle (regarded as a bird of good omen), Falco ponticerianus.
v.t. = ćheʼonā, q.v.
s.m. Curd; a sweetmeat made of curds ( = ćhenā-barā).
s.m. A small kind of cymbal.
v.n. To be torn, be slit, be rent, be cleft or riven (as the eye of a needle, &c.), to be worn out, to be rubbed or worn away, to fray, to fret (as cloth); to become bad, to turn (as milk)
s.f. (prov.) = ćhināl, q.v.

ety: ćheṅć˚ = Prk. छेच्छ(इ) = S. छेत्स्य(ति), fut. of rt. छिद् (used for the pres.)
def: v.t. To mince, back, chop, pound, &c.
s.f. = ćheʼonī, q.v.
s.f. Detention, stoppage, interruption, restraint, arrest; sequestration, confiscation.
s.m. Detention, &c. ( = ćhenk, q v.).
v.t. To interrupt, to interfere with, cross the path of; to detain, prevent, stop, retain, bar, restrain, arrest; — to cut out, to cross or score out, to cancel; — to surpass, excel
subs: v.t. (intens.) To stop, &c.; to sequester, to confiscate.

def: s.m. One who detains or stops, &c.; a confiscator.

s.f. Touching, touch, handling, passing the hand over; meddling with, molesting, molestation, provocation, excitement, irritating, irritation, vexing, vexation, worrying, annoying; — touching up, stirring up, making active; action, activity, stir; incitement, stimulus, fillip; — dalliance, flirtation, amorous intercourse or skirmishing; jest, fun
subs: ćheṛ-khānī, s.f. Touching, touch, &c. ( = ćheṛ); — stricture, animadversion; mutual action or exertion; interchange (of compliments, &c.); attack and counter-attack; — tightening the strings or screws (of an instrument), tuning (an instrument).

def: s.m. A goat (see ćherī).
s.m. Touch; provocation, &c. ( = ćher, q.v.).
s.f. A she-goat.
v.n. To be troubled with indigestion, to have a bad digestion (cf. ćheṛnā).

ety: ćheṛ˚ = Prk. छेड(इ) or छेडे(इ), fr. S. p.p.p. क्षिप्त
def: v.t. To touch, lay the hand on, pass the hand over; to meddle with, molest, interrupt, disturb, trouble, annoy, tease, torment, worry, irritate, vex, excite, provoke; to touch up, stir up, incite, stimulate, jog, urge, spur; — to question closely, or searchingly, or strictly, to call to account, take to task, censure, rebuke; to address unseemly language to, to abuse, insult; to laugh at, quiz, deride; — to touch upon, to begin; — to tune (an instrument preparatory to playing on it), to strike the chords, to begin playing.

ety: ćheṭ˚ = Prk. छित्त(इ)? fr. S. क्षिप्त p.p.p. rt. क्षिप्; cf. ćhednā
def: v.t. To break, crush, bruise, pound; to beat, hammer, flatten.

s.m. Landed property, land, soil, a field ( = khet), plantation; a battle-field; a sphere of action; — place, region, country; a sacred spot or district, a place of pilgrimage (as Benares, &c.), &c. (see kshetr); — a geometrical figure, a plane surface
subs: s.m. A man employed to guard fields from depredation; — proprietor of the soil, landlord, &c. ( = bhūmiyā, q.v.)
s.m. Superficial contents or area (of a field or a plane surface, or of a geometrical figure)
s.f. The boundaries of a field, or of a sacred place.

def: s.m. The owner, or the cultivator of a field, &c., an agriculturist, a husbandman; — husband.
s.m. = ćhatrī, q.v.

ety: ćhew+lī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ल or र+इका
def: s.f. The Indian butter-tree, Bassia butyracea.
s.m. A cut, incision; mark, stroke; a piece (of wood, &c.) cut off, a chip
subs: (-meṅ or -par), To make an incision (in), to mark, to line out.

def: s.m. A stroke or line (in writing), space, dash, comma, stop; — (dialec.) inoculation, vaccination
subs: (-ko), To make a line, a dash, or stop, &c.; — to vaccinate.

ety: ćhew+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः, and ˚ur = Prk. उल्लो = S. ऊलः
def: s.m. Skin, cuticle.

ety: chew˚ = Prk. छेअ (इ), fr. S. छेद, rt. छिद्
def: v.t. To cut, make an incision in, to lance, to mark; to pierce or bleed a tār-tree, to extract tāṛī or palm-juice; to notch (a millstone); — to shake, stir, disturb.
s.f. An instrument for piercing with; a tool for notching millstones, &c.; a piercer, a chisel, a punch; (Mech.) a wedge.
intj. Fy! foh! faugh! — ćhī-ćhī, s.f. Dirt, filth, excrement; — (colloq.) an epithet applied to a class of Eurasians (by whom the expression ćhī is commonly used); — intj. Fy! shame! &c. (see ćhī).
adj. (f. -ī), Shallow, of little depth.
s.f. Shallowness.
s.f. The play of ducks and drakes
subs: To play at ducks and drakes.

ety: Prk. छुच्छअडओ (with w inserted); S. तुच्छक+र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Light, vain, airy, petty, trifling, frivolous, puerile, childish, shallow, superficial; tawdry, showy, ostentatious; low, mean, insignificant, contemptible.
adj. = ćhićhorā, q.v.
s.m. Lightness, airiness, frivolity, puerility; shallowness; meanness; show, display, &c.
s.m. = ćhićṛā, q.v.
s.m. Skin, membrane, &c. ( = ćhićṛā, q.v.); the skinny part of meat, tough and uneatable part of flesh
subs: To flay; to tear to pieces.

def: adj. (f. -ī), ćhićhorā, q.v.
s.m. Skin, cuticle, integument, the cellular membrane that covers the surface of the body; slough (of a wound), crust (of a sore); the prepuce, foreskin; entrails; offal.
adj. Skinny, lean.
adj. Thin, not close (as cloth, &c.), sparse, straggling, widely separated, &c. ( = ćhidrā, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Pierced, bored, perforated.
v.t. To cause to pierce; to perforate; to get pierced or perforated ( = ćhidwānā).
v.n. To be pierced, to be bored
adj. (f. -ī), Containing gaps or openings, thin (as cloth, a crop, &c.), not closely woven; sparse, meagre; straggling, widely separated, wide apart (as the legs of a person or animal; = ćhīdā).
s.m. A hole, orifice, opening, aperture, a crack; a defect, fault, flaw; weakness, weak point; — adj. Pierced, perforated (see ćhidrā; chīdā).
v.t. To place at wide intervals, to spread out; — v.n. To walk with the legs far apart; — ćhidrā-kar ćalnā, v.n. Idem.
s.f. = S چهدرتو छिद्रत्व ćhidratva, s.m. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
s.m. = S چهدرتا छिद्रता ćhidratā, s.f. The state of being perforated, the quality of giving space, holes, or openings; the quality of being pervaded by everything.
part. Pierced, perforated, having holes, gaps, or openings.
v.t. To cause to be pierced, to havo or get pierced or bored.
adj. Seventy-six.
s.f. Loss, waste, wane, diminution, ebb (of tide, &c.); destruction, decay, wasting or wearing away; — ćhīj-baṭṭā, ćhīj-ćhapaṭ, s.m. Tare and tret.
v.t. To cause to waste or wear away; to decrease, to lessen.

ety: ćhīj˚ = Prk. छिज्ज(इ) = S. छिद्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. छिद्
def: v.n. To decrease, dwindle, diminish, wane; to decay, waste away, fade, wither, pine away, become emaciated, to wear away (by attrition, &c.), to be effaced or removed
s.m. = ćhīṅkā, q.v.
s.m. Sneezing (see ćhīṅknā).
v.n. To be cut or crossed out, to be cancelled; to be detained, be stopped, be arrested, &c.; to be confiscated, be sequestrated.
s.f. A switch, a stick, a wand ( = ćhaṛī).
s.f. Parings, scrapings (of rind or peel), clippings, shavings, sortings.
v.t. = ćhilwānā, q.v.

ety: ćhilkā or ćhāl+aurī = Prk. उल्लिआ = S. ऊल+इका
def: s.f. A blister ( = ćhālā); a whitlow, a felon.
s.m. Skin, cuticle, epidermis; bark, rind; shell, peel, husk, hull; crust, scale
subs: (-kā), To blanch, peel, husk, shell, decorticate.

def: s.m. = ćhilkā.
s.m. The husk of ćanā, q.v.

ety: prob. akin to ćholṅā, q.v.; or fr. छिद्र
def: v.t. To skin, excoriate, peel, pare, bark, to scratch, scrape, erase; — (fig.) to ask the same thing again and again; to make a nice point of
v.n. To be excoriated; to be peeled, be shelled, be husked; to be erased, be scraped, be scratched out (see ćhīlnā).
s.f. Peeling, paring, husking, &c.; — price paid for peeling or husking, &c.
v.t. To cause to be skinned, or peeled, &c.; to have (a thing) peeled, pared, or scraped, &c.
s.f. A pod, legume; a bean; green pease, &c. in the pod (cf. maṭar).
adj. Wasted, worn away; emaciated, thin, feeble; delicate, slender, slight, little; poor, miserable.
s.m. Snatching, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: ćhīnā-jhapṭī, s.f. Snatching, taking by force
ćhīnā-ćhīnī, s.f. Mutual snatching and tugging; pulling backwards and forwards; scrambling, scramble (syn. kashmakash)

ety: ćhī˚ = Prk. छिव(इ) or छिह(इ) = S. स्पृश(ति), rt. स्पृश्; cf. ćhīhnā
def: v.t. (dialec.) = ćhūnā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.), To sting, to bite (cf. ćheʼonā).
s.m. Snatching, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: ćhīnā-jhapṭī, s.f. Snatching, taking by force
ćhīnā-ćhīnī, s.f. Mutual snatching and tugging; pulling backwards and forwards; scrambling, scramble (syn. kashmakash)

def: s.m. & adv., contrac. fr. ćhin-ek, q.v.s.v. ćhin.
v.t. To winnow, to sift.
v.n. To blow the nose; — to go away offended.
s.f. A harlot, a prostitute, a wanton, a drab, a trull
s.m. A dissolute character, a profligate, a fornicator, a rake.
s.f. Harlotry, prostitution; fornication, adultery

ety: the preceding, with S. इका for कः
def: s.f. = ćhināl, q.v.
s.f. Harlotry, prostitution; fornication, adultery
